title: Scheduling Policies
text:
In Kubernetes versions before v1.23, a scheduling policy can be used to specify the
predicates
and
priorities
process. For example, you can set a scheduling policy by
running
kube-scheduler --policy-config-file <filename>
or
kube-scheduler --policy-configmap <ConfigMap>
.
This scheduling policy is not supported since Kubernetes v1.23. Associated flags
policy-config-file
,
policy-configmap
,
policy-configmap-namespace
and
use-legacy-policy-config
are also not supported. Instead, use the
Scheduler Configuration
to achieve similar behavior.
